title: salix fluviatilis var. tenerrima
intro: Salix fluviatilis var. tenerrima, also known as the Willow-herb, is a plant species belonging to the Salicaceae family. It is primarily used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections, due to its high levels of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Salix fluviatilis var. tenerrima can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of conditions, from colds and flu to digestive issues and skin problems.
